text:
Why are we forced to deal with automated returns?

I primary deal in brand new auto parts, so I expect returns but ebay trying to be like Amazon is getting irritating. I am sick of this Per eBay's policy, this return has been automatically approved and we've asked the buyer to send the item back. Usually the return is for a reason I can fix or clarify without the return even needing to be done, but being its approved automatically I have zero control. When I try to message the buyer to clarify, I get ignored, because most buyers don't even pay attention to their accounts. I need to be able to message the buyer before the return is approved. I get a lot of returns because they changed their mind or found it cheaper. So I have been forced into free retuns, so I loose a huge chunk of money each time they change their mind. Half the time its because they found same item $2 cheaper somewhere else, so I eat shipping and return costs so they can save a couple bucks, when I would have refunded them the difference instead. Also a VIN should be required for ALL auto parts purchases to fall under free returns. Because I have found out most people have no idea about their vehicle and guess when ordering parts, so they may get the wrong year, or engine size, etc.... This would also stop unnecessary returns. Customers do not read, they see a title, first picture and then buy. Its massively irritating being blamed for the customers failure to understand, read or buying with assumed information. Literally all my negative feedback is from customers failing to understand, but ebays automated policy denys my ask for removal.
title: Why are we forced to deal with automated returns?

I bought some phones from when ebay had 20% off in February from a seller I had been buying from since 2023. Purchased 50 to 60 plus phones from him sometimes more than $4000 each purchase. text:
RETURNS unjust for seller due to "buyers remorse"

I’m writing to formally complain and request a review of a recent return that was approved even though the buyer simply “changed their mind” after receiving the items. I sold two items that were listed as Free Shipping. Shipping cost me $12+ to send to the buyer. After delivery, the buyer decided they no longer wanted the items and initiated a return. eBay approved the return, and I then had to pay additional shipping for the items to be returned to me as well. This is completely unjust for the seller. I fulfilled the order correctly, shipped on time, and the items were delivered. There was no issue with the listing, no defect, and no seller error—only buyer’s remorse. The buyer had no obligation to purchase the items, but once they did, the current process effectively makes it my obligation to absorb multiple shipping charges because they changed their mind. That results in a financial penalty for doing everything right. I’m requesting that eBay: Review this return decision and the reason code used, and Provide a resolution that reimburses or credits the outbound and/or return shipping costs that I was forced to absorb due to buyer remorse, and Clarify what seller protections apply in cases where returns are approved solely because the buyer changed their mind.
title: RETURNS unjust for seller due to "buyers remorse"

text:
Buying grocery items

There is a common grocery item that I use a lot of, and it seemed too expensive in the local grocery store. So I used to but it in bulk from Amazon or Walmart, saving about 25%! But then the price went up, a lot, so I was looking for other ways to buy in bulk and save some money. I found the item on EBay. But no seller seemed to have more than 2 of the item. Yet each seller offered the item at the low price I was used to paying, shipping included, even though I was not buying in bulk like I had with Walmart (which still offered the item, but at a much higher price). So I ordered 2 of the item from each of several sites on EBay. The items have arrived and everything seems fine. But the items have arrived in open paper bags with handles or sealed cardboard shipping boxes, all Walmart branded. Not sure if they've arrived via USPS, UPS, or private courier. Anyone know what is going on here? Any chance these items were stolen from Walmart and being resold?
title: Buying grocery items

text:
Items listed forever and nobody cares

Since years now I see the same used items listed forever at a fixed stellar price. As such fixed prices are coming from nowhere, guess what, nobody is buying them. So such items stay there forever and ever. As a result when you're looking for something on Ebay, you need to struggle through pages and pages and pages of the same items you see again and again since months or even years. I suppose that those vendors basically do not need that money, so they leave those items there just in case one day someone who is not aware of what the real market is accepts to give a huge amount of money away with no reason. It might happen, I know, and it does, like once in a decade. Leaving those items ageing there costs nothing to vendors as Ebay is not asking them anything to keep them listed. This makes completely no sense to me, what's the point, I really can't see who is taking advantage from such a business model. People don't sell, buyers don't buy, Ebay don't earn. Mistery! To me, used items should be listed as auctions only, as easy as that. It's up to the market to set the fair price, not to vendors. Especially because most of the time they don't even have idea of what they are selling, how on Earth are they supposed to set a price? New items is a completely different story. Can anyone (possibly from Ebay) clarify what's behind all of this?
title: Items listed forever and nobody cares

text:
Accessibility Concern: No Support Options for Sellers With Hearing Impairments

I’m hoping to get clarification or guidance regarding an accessibility issue I’ve encountered with eBay’s seller support system. At the moment, eBay appears to offer live agent assistance only through phone calls. For sellers with hearing impairments—or anyone who cannot effectively use voice calls—this creates a major barrier to receiving help, resolving account issues, or appealing seller performance evaluations. In my case, I need to submit a formal dispute regarding my seller level and account restrictions, but the only path to a live representative is by phone. Written communication options (email, chat, or secure messaging) are either unavailable or extremely limited, which leaves me without an accessible way to address urgent account matters. This raises a few important questions: - What support channels does eBay provide for sellers who cannot use phone-based communication due to hearing impairments or other disabilities? - Is there an established accessibility pathway for account appeals or performance disputes? - If not, how can sellers in this situation receive equal access to support and resolution? eBay’s marketplace relies on fairness and accessibility for all users, and I believe this is an area where additional support options are needed. Any guidance from mentors or moderators would be greatly appreciated. Thank you.
title: Accessibility Concern: No Support Options for Sellers With Hearing Impairments

I am a seller who's been on the platform longer, I have sold more items, I have almost 100x more items listed than they do, I have a 100% positive feedback record (they have almost 70 complaints on their account, I have 1 neutral that should been removed as the buyer didn't understand the product) ..... Am I just being a Karen and jealous? If I was I wouldn't be writing this lol.. my issue is I have applied to do ebay live streams twice.. never been responded to. I am writing this awaiting resolution on a major account issue I have been not responded to by 5 different reps currently (IE I am told I would get a follow up that never occurs, have to call back in, rinse, repeat).... and this person who has a smaller account, less items for sale, and been on the platform much less time than I have... gets an puff article blasted to every person on ebay, their account advertised to millions for free, and ebay live access. The only difference between this person and I? They have a tiny youtube channel.... I only sell on ebay to hundreds of thousands of customers. I guess ebay wants the few dozens people a youtube channel might attract over loyalty to SELLERS WHO HAVE BEEN ON HERE SINCE THE 1990's!!! 10 years ago I was bigger than accounts on here that are now infamous for criminal activity. ebay continues to advertise them, give them access to things they won't even acknowledge are real (IE much lower fee's that allow my direct competitors to advertise consignment under cost), and here I am... busting my a** every day... called a "unicorn" by tech support because of my sales record / feedback score... and treated like I will literally never matter to this company. /rant..... text:
Request for Moderator Guidance – MC999 Permanent Suspension on 2007 Account (diamondintherough_1984)

Hello Community Team and Moderators, I am seeking guidance regarding a recent MC999 permanent suspension placed on my account, diamondintherough_1984, which has been active since 2007 with approximately 38 successful transactions and 100% positive feedback. I fully understand the importance of marketplace safety and the need for protective measures. However, I would appreciate clarification and, if possible, a further internal review of my case due to the specific circumstances involved. The suspension appears to have been triggered after I created a secondary account and conducted a transaction involving one of my own listings. The sole purpose of this transaction was to test whether my American Express card would process successfully via PayPal before making a legitimate high-value purchase from another seller. The listing used was a duplicated version of an existing item I was already selling (Qatar Airways pyjamas), briefly modified for the test. The listing was not intended for public sale, was live only momentarily, and there was no third-party buyer involvement. The item was dispatched using Royal Mail postage purchased through eBay and was delivered back to me the following day. There was no loss to any buyer, no feedback manipulation, and no attempt to artificially inflate selling metrics. Shortly thereafter, I received notification that my account was permanently suspended due to activity believed to pose a risk to the eBay community. I submitted an appeal explaining the full context and acknowledging that the test transaction may have triggered internal risk flags. While I respect eBay’s policies, I am hopeful that a moderator may be able to advise whether a senior manual review could reconsider the proportionality of a permanent suspension in this case, particularly given: The long-standing account history (since 2007) 100% positive feedback No prior warnings No third-party financial harm No unresolved disputes Additionally, funds from the transaction remain on hold, and I would appreciate clarification regarding their scheduled release timeline or whether they will be returned to the original payment method. I sincerely regret the error in judgment that led to this situation and would welcome the opportunity to demonstrate compliance going forward. If any moderator is able to review or escalate this case for further assessment, I would be extremely grateful. Thank you for your time and any guidance you may be able to provide. Kind regards, Alistair
title: Request for Moderator Guidance – MC999 Permanent Suspension on 2007 Account (diamondintherough_1984)

I want to sell my Alienware UltraWide Monitor. It is still in very good condition. I still have the original shipping box it came in. my plan is to pack the monitor in the original shipping box in exactly the way it was shipped to me. (undamaged.) My concern is that it can be broken, (especially the screen being cracked, etc., ) during shipping. I know I can purchase insurance, but I also know that insurance usually first looks for a way to deny my claim because I am somehow negligent in how it was packed for shipping. I am assuming that the best method to pack the monitor is to use the original shipping box as it was originally shipped to me with no damage. I would like to hear from sellers who have purchased UPS shipping insurance, and how UPS handled their claim. Should I be concerned that UPS will use any miniscule excuse to weasel out of paying my claim should the monitor be damaged during shipping? I could, of course, only offer to sell the monitor using local pickup only, but that dramatically lowers the number of potential buyers, even considering that I live in the Los Angeles area. text:
Shipping options when listing and after selling

When I list an item, many times I get the option to ship via eBay standard envelope or USPS standard envelope. My item fits the parameters given. Once I sell the item and the buyer pays for shipping option I provided, I no longer have that option for shipping. WHY? Why does eBay change my shipping options? If it is given as an option when listing, why is it not given as an option after selling?? Because of this, I AM LOSING MONEY. I am so frustrated with eBay! Thinking of going back to WHATNOT!!! or NIKNAX!!
title: Shipping options when listing and after selling

text:
Is it already too late for ebay to grow with Livestreaming?

When ebay first announced it was getting involved in Livestreaming, there were quite a few predictions that WhatNot was doomed....a scrappy upstart would have no chance against the size , experience and sheer financial power of the much larger ebay. Of course, that assumed that ebay was capable of rolling out livestreaming at something other than a glacial pace (pre-global warming glacial pace, at that) Many of us on this Board are a bit older (livestreaming is more popular with the younger set), and some tend to be dismissive of livestreaming (and anything involving video sales generally), but if you wonder why some of us are absolutely baffled at ebay's failure to grow its Livestreaming business, take a look at this report from WhatNot to see just how much buying and selling is tied to livestreaming (on WhatNot and elsewhere) and how little impact ebay has had in the space. Quick moves by ebay could have not only made ebay the dominant player in this space, but could also have provided real GMV growth with much less need to extract more and more revenue from sellers through First Party Ads. Instead, at ebay Open ebay talked about how they've added a few categories and a few more sellers to their Livestreams, and how well it is doing. Like every site and sales method, livestreaming (on WhatNot or elsewhere) has pros and cons. But it is clear: Livestreaming is not a flash in the pan sort of thing, and it could have been a major agent of growth for ebay. Instead, it is hard to imagine ebay making any bold, effective moves in this space, because other sites have grown rapidly. And livestreaming on these other sites isn't limited to sneakers or trading cards....a huge variety of stuff is being sold, including the sort of stuff many people used to think of as "you will only find that on ebay). Will ebay EVER really grow its Livestreaming component? Or is it already too late, and Livestreaming on ebay is largely going to be confined to certain categories and especially to going live at conventions etc? https://www.valueaddedresource.net/whatnot-2-billion-gmv-state-of-livestream-selling-report/
title: Is it already too late for ebay to grow with Livestreaming?

text:
The FREE shipping EXPERIENCE... Not a podcast...

Hello, my name is Simon, Am very new to E-Bay, and am from East coast Canada (Montreal), discouraged in many ways but am trying to persevere, trying new things, watching videos of big time sellers explaining their tactics and I've tried a few things so far. In a video I saw, it was stated that offering FREE SHIPPING was one of the prime drivers of sales and doing so would likely create traffic to my postings. Having gone thru this: What your buyer paid Item subtotal C $50.00 Shipping C $13.82 GST/HST* C $8.30 Order total** C $72.12 What you earned Order total C $72.12 eBay collected from buyer GST/HST -C $8.30 Selling costs Transaction fees -C $11.78 Shipping label -C $14.66 Ad Fee General -C $10.19 Order earnings C $27.19 I decided I would attempt FREE SHIPPING in the following way: Say my item price is 50.00$ and my shipping range is 12.00$ to 30.00$. (from Canada Post) I figured, since I need to pay for the labels, pay the taxes on the sum of the item + shipping cost, being those 2 are controllable for me (as opposed to final sale fees) I would use the following (flawed) logic: My item listing price asked (50.00$), adding the maximum range of shipping (30.00$) would make the listing total to (80.00$), then offering FREE SHIPPING, BUT also open the OFFERS to potential buyers, once an offer is made or interest is shown, I figured I could see WHERE the buyer is, calculate the shipping precisely and then allow the offer to match my base price (50.00$) PLUS the cost of actual shipping (say on average 15.00$) then allowing me to ACCEPT an offer of 65.00$ on the listing. My logic being: I don't wanna gouge or make profit on shipping, I just want to be able to control the total expenses so that I don't end up losing 50% of my sales total to FEES and taxes (as in given example above.) This way if a customer makes an OFFER and he's from Timbuktu where shipping would cost the maximum possible within the range shown by E-Bay, I can just decline the offer if it's going to incur me too much ROI loss. I would then decide if I want to ACCEPT an offer and lower my ROI a bit. Being am from Canada, am FORCED to allow people to BUY it NOW from me even if they are all the way to the West coast and costs can go wild as in a range of 12.35$ to 180.63$. If a customer BUYS and ends up paying the 180.63$ delivery, I get NAILED with insane TAXES, destroying my ROI and even making me have a loss without any control over this. Baffling to me is the fact, using USPS, to ship to California, is FIXED price and costs up to 8 times less than me shipping from Montreal (east coast) to Vancouver (west coast). Sorry if am rambling to some, am new to all this, trying to apply logic the way I see it. So, all this said, 4 days ago I tried this, bundled items together (I sell LEGOs mostly), activated FREE shipping, added amounts to my listings, opened the OFFERS feature, I did REMOVE all Promotion percentages (as a test to get a pure r...
title: The FREE shipping EXPERIENCE... Not a podcast...

eBay Live Video Sales Now Include Non Focus Categories?

kyle@ebay I have noticed that ebay seller and influencer Anna Packer has run a few eBay Lives now, today's featuring women's hats----not luxury brands, not street wear, not authenticated stuff, just regular womens hats (vintage and newer) https://www.ebay.com/ebaylive/events/vhIDCXhTWx4AIW40 Until Anna, I believe all the Lives have been tied to Focus Categories, such as sneakers, comics, trading cards etc. So has ebay quietly expanded Lives into core category inventory? Can you provide insight into any sort of timeline here...when can we expect to see further expansion and additional approved sellers like Anna? While I know Lives are not everyone's cup of tea, I'm sure there are more sellers who would like to have the opportunity to run Live sales directly on ebay, rather than WhatNot, Facebook etc. Especially with Q4 right around the corner. So, any insights you can provide would be much appreciated.

text:
Has eBay shadow banned my account?

Has ebay shadow banned my account? I know the clothing market is dying a painful death, but this is beyond ridiculous, 11 days no sales. I have around £12000 of stuff listed. Last August I had £4300 in sales, this month I’ve taken £320. in comparison I’ve taken more than this on 3 other marketplaces. Meanwhile other platforms perform well, notably Depop, Vinted and Whatnot eBay has dropped to my 4th marketplace. Barely any views or watchers, no send offers. My items are definitely competitively priced, others are selling for similar or higher prices than mine. I recently went from top rated seller to below standard due to late deliveries (notmyfault) which has put my fees up higher, but still no sales?! I listed a few items on a private account and all had multiple views , watchers and 2 sold. these were listed for the same price on my business account for months. (I also delete items daily as they sell on other marketplaces) should I start a new account?
title: Has eBay shadow banned my account?

text:
Protection against fraudulent buyers

eBay - you need to change this!!! This is so sickening!! No protection for sellers. Typical bulk buyer: buys 2 9-card lots of a prospect and asks me to send both in one package - so I do and they get there fast. 4 days later puts in full refund request - saying cards are ALL damaged due to my packaging. LOOK at my feedbacks . . I get HUGE praise for the way I package my auctions - I go above and beyond. eBay sides with me and no refund. Days later he tries again - saying he didn't get 2nd package. Not only do I have the emails from him asking me to send both in one package, in his photos you see more than 9 cards - proving that he received both lots. I point this out to eBay and the buyer - he immediately withdraws 2nd refund request. Caught him totally lying and should be end of the story - nope. He puts this long, dishonest negative feedback. Since eBay sided with me - I figure they'll fix it as soon as they get my request. . nope. Buyers are allowed to leave their thoughts. OK!!!! Let me leave negative feedback for the buyer . . and that's all he is by the way . .only a buyer. . .hasn't sold on eBay in well over a year. They buy on eBay where they can get away with this garbage - - and then they turn around and grade and trade/sell at card shows or whatnot. eBay - - you better protect us fewer sellers that you retain. Why would anyone sell on here if they aren't protected - - especially when liar is caught red-handed????
title: Protection against fraudulent buyers

text:
Bold Listing Fee Charged without my consent

Im writing on here because sellers clearly have no power and are not important to ebay. On two of my listings, maybe more, each one $30 items ebay without my consent changed my listings to Bold Listing Fee and charged me $8. I am a stay at home mom who supplements my household income selling on ebay and Im lucky to make between $500-$1000 a month doing this. Im careful when I list and there is absolutly no way I wouldve checked this box even by accident. It tells you at the bottom the fee will be $4. Why would anyone bold list for a $30 notebook. I am sick and tired of ebay crapping on Americans and sellers. In order to get help for this I have to communicate with someone from India who could care less about me and my American problems. Who probably thinks I am just being a "Karen." Its not suprising ebay is losing business to Mercari, Poshmark and Whatnot. And because they are losing this business they are making us pay for it. Just last week I had to OPT OUT of ebay stealing my shipping discount and passing it along to customers. READ every single email you get from them because they are robbing us blind at every turn.
title: Bold Listing Fee Charged without my consent

ZOSI CCTV

I purchased a ZOSI 8CH 5MP Lite DVR 1080P Outdoor CCTV Security Camera System Kit Night Vision and ZOSI Security Camera Cable 150ft BNC Power Video Wire Extension Cord for CCTV and I left feedback after I tried both products. I downloaded the AVSS for my PC, used it and soon had odd "popups" occur. I had a problem with setting up user accounts on my DVR, chatted with support (was kind of difficult). Friendly and helpful and asked me to leave feedback (again) for product and mention the name of support agent to help their status for customer support with their company for review or whatnot and then I realized I was leaving feedback to a different model DVR even after they requested me to send pic of the QR scan sticker on my DVR at the beginning of the support chat. I left feedback for the extension cables a few weeks ago as well and received a "feedback request" from ZOSI 2 days ago. I saw that one other person had the same problem with leaving second feedback but my main concern is the "POPUPS" that occurred immediately after installing and using the AVSS for PC. Any information or tips will be greatly appreciated.
title: ZOSI CCTV

text:
Is it even worth trying to continue here?

So I’m a new seller. I put up close to 40 auctions my first go around. My mistake was I scheduled most of them and did so have g them all close with in like a 2 day period. I sell higher end glass pieces primarily so packing in the correct box size and protective wrapping is paramount. I ran out of supplies which caused a day delay to the process of getting everything out. But all in all I had 7 orders that went over the 3 days before over due. 4 went out the day they hit over due. And the remaining 3 were customers either asking me to ship at a later day because they wouldn’t be home. Or a buyer out of Australia mistaking the global shipping program with the eBay’s international shipping. That customer is a seller with like 100,000 orders u see her belt. I was taking my direction from her trying to find how to ship this large package she said I could but could find it. Finally I figured out she was mistaken the services. We decided to cancel the order and I’d relist it for her to buy after signing up for global shipping. also as of now the other two delayed request have been shipped out as per the customers request. but I get restricted by eBay. First I just provide tracking, done. then they all must show proof delivery, done. mind you now I’ve been waiting 5 days to get paid, it’s the first of the month. I just sold almost 3k worth of glass. the day all the deliveries show delivered. Instead of sticking to their word. I get more nonsense. show proof of ID, they want cards and paperwork. Then to make it even more ridiculous. I need to show receipts for all the items I sold? I mean I can I have them but why on earth would they need this? They believe it make believe glass? Doesn’t the zero complaints and the positives reviews tell the true story here? what’s next, are they going to ask me to send them my birth certificate, then potentially a permission slip from my wife showing her approval of selling glass on eBay? Seema eBay frankly doesn’t want sellers here? Or at least me. And I got to say whatnot and other marketplaces sure as heck roll out the red carpet treatment to new Sellers. Acting and showing they are thrilled for us to be there. is there a point in continuing to play the game of provide and then wait for more waiting? Irritated that eBay treats themselves like customers and everyone else like the problem with in their business operation.
title: Is it even worth trying to continue here?

text:
I won a giveaway from a seller on whatnot and sold it on ebay.what is cost of goods for it for taxes

I won a giveaway from a live auction seller from whatnot. It was valued at $60 when i won it and i ended up selling it for 35$ on ebay. Now its income tax season and im calculating gross income. Im trying to figure out what the cost of goods of this item be if i won it for free from someone. My two options i was thinking of was $0 and 60$. $0 because i got it for free and did not purchase it. 60$ based on (FMV) Fair Market Value of the time i won and recieved the item, and it was worth 60 at the time.
title: I won a giveaway from a seller on whatnot and sold it on ebay.what is cost of goods for it for taxes

text:
Slightly Different Product

What would you do if you bought a product, and when it arrived it didn't match with what was posted on ebay? For example, a signed hockey puck with a Fanatics Hologram for authentication. The posted item was signed with a thinner pen and was much more attractive. The one that arrived was a thicker pen or marker and didn't seem as nice. The hologram codes are different; yet, they both can be authenticated to the same hockey player. The inscription is the same. Thoughts? Advice? I contacted the seller... ... but, have yet to reply. What should I expect? There is nothing in the original post on ebay that says stock photo or you may received a slightly different puck.
title: Slightly Different Product

text:
List of User IDs that do not pay

Does Ebay have a list of user IDs that frequently do not pay for their items? Love to have a list so we as sellers can block. For all the high fees we pay for Ebay there should be something that blocks these users from locking up our items for sale for 4 days as we wait for a non payment. This is why discord, fanatics collect, etc are becoming more attractive with less fees. Also, we should be allowed to write NEGATIVE FEEDBACK as sellers. Ebay fix this now.
title: List of User IDs that do not pay

text:
Why does eBay punish all users for the small % of unpaid auctions?

I’ve been buying for 15 years on this platform and not once have I failed to pay an auction. Now, my card is forcibly automatically charged 1 hour after EVEN IF THE SELLER DOESNT WANT THAT because 1% of users fail to pay. WHAT IF I WANT TO WIN ANOTHER AUCTION FROM THE SELLER INSTEAD OF PAYING SHIPPING MULTIPLE TIMES AND MAKE THEM REPACKAGE MULTIPLE TIMES? WHAT IF I WANT TO ADD MORE BUY IT NOWS TO THAT ORDER AND CHECK OUT ALL AT ONCE INSTEAD OF PAYING SHIPPING MULTIPLE TIMES AND MAKE THEM REPACKAGE MULTIPLE TIMES? WHAT IF I WANT TO USE FINANCING OPTIONS INSTEAD OF FORCING A DIRECT PAYMENT? “Oh, just do that in that hour” WHAT IF IM AT WORK WHEN THE AUCTION ENDS? WHAT IF THE SELLERS AUCTIONS END MORE THAN AN HOUR APART? WHAT IF IM SLEEPING WHEN THE AUCTION ENDS? WHAT IF THE SELLER DOESNT WANT THIS STUPID SYSTEM AND YET YOU FORCE IT ON EVERYONE WHY ARE YOU MANDATING PUNISHMENT THOSE WHO HAVE NEVER FAILED TO FOR THE SMALL FRACTION ACCOUNTS THAT DONT? This product development team needs to get their head out of their 🫏 it’s like them and the engineers are in a competition for who can sabotage the company more by making the customer experience as terrible as possible through pure incompetence. My spending has gone from thousands a month to maybe a couple hundred dollars out of pure frustration with how much this messes up how I make my orders. I’m now forced to do 1 item at a time and nothing that would be 6 month finance since they don’t allow you to set that up. and to think it could all be avoided if they WOULDNT PUNISH EVERYONE FOR THE SMALL FRACTION OF BUYERS WHO DONT PAY and instead implement a…wait for it…strike system. Wow what a novel concept that 10,000 other companies do. Don’t follow through on an auction? Now you have forced payments on your account for a year. All new accounts have forced payments for their first x number of purchases or 90 days. This solution took me 10 seconds to think of. But no, let’s instead make everything as annoying and inconvenient for buyers and sellers alike and GIVE NO OPTION TO SELLERS TO TURN IT OFF. Last question, and totally unrelated, why is Fanatics and PWCC wiping the floor with eBay? 🤔
title: Why does eBay punish all users for the small % of unpaid auctions?

text:
Refunds

text:
Inaccurate Payments & Purchases

Hey, I've been experiencing some weird activity on my eBay account. My situation is, I own a sports card & memorabilia store, and upgraded my eBay & PayPal accounts to business accounts. Since doing so my accounts have had issues. Error messages, Purchases charged to wrong account, and Questionable purchases that weren't verified. It has been a big headache as I've had to trace back every purchase to see which orders had a discrepancy. Has anyone had this happen to them before, and if so how were you able to get this resolved? Why does eBay separate each order when purchasing from multiple sellers? Example: In the past, I'll order 50 items from 30 different sellers and have 30 different transactions in my bank account. Isn't it obvious to eBay it's better for businesses to have the orders process in a single transaction through bank accounts. Then have the invoice details of that single transaction with a break down of all the sellers & orders included. Amazon, Fanatics, and other companies do it. Why not eBay? Thank you for any help you can provide, Tony Great Lakes Sports Cards Amherst, Ohio
title: Inaccurate Payments & Purchases

text:
EBay bias

This is a warning for those of you who sell trading cards on the EBay platform and think you are protected by the new authentication process EBay has set up to protect sellers and buyers. It's total **bleep** if you are a seller. A buyer purchased a BGS 9.9 Triple Threads auto card that went through professional authentication. The card passed inspection and the buyer received the card. He asked for a refund because he did not think the card matched the listing and took issue with the Beckett Grading Service grade on the corners of the card. I filed a complaint against him and never heard anything back from eBay. The card is on its way back to me and I have been told to issue a refund. Can you believe that crap? I have been a top rated seller for over 12 years and this clown has been on EBay for 5 months. If Football Fanatics ever sets up a similar platform I have to believe it will not have the anti seller mentality that EBay has. Lesson learned...it's ok to be deceitful if you are a buyer but not as a seller.
title: EBay bias

text:
Removal of Authentic Items

Hello to all of you fanatics, I'm really hoping some of you fine people here may be of some help to a fellow sports fan, albeit a very confused and perplexed one at the moment. I will try to keep this short and concise but went into the matter in greater detail over there, before arriving here via suggestion. This post continues to get flagged as spam because, I think, I'm posting a link to that conversation. It can be found under "Authentic Item Being Removed" in The eBay Community forum. I have several commemorative patches for the Seahawks that I purchased at their official team store (called The Pro Shop) at the then CenturyLink Field and currently Lumen Field in Seattle back in 2018. The patches were sold individually, with no packaging for $5 and were often applied onto jerseys. I have since listed and sold multiple of these to completion with satisfied buyers. I woke up yesterday to a message from ebay stating that the patches violate ebay's "Counterfeit item policy", to not relist them or "Further Action May be Taken Against Me"...aka suspension or termination of my account. I know however to absolute certainty they are legit and genuine and would be happy to share any and all images or information to help prove it to be so. I can't access the removed listing because I get redirected to ebay's home page via an error message, but I have the item number for a completed listing that is identical to the one that was flagged/removed. It is a commemorative Paul Gardner Allen (PGA) patch in recognition of his passing and owner/savior of the Seahawks in Seattle. Here is that item number 324831023198. I reached out to Customer Service via chat but the person claimed that my item was deemed to be counterfeit and essentially, that's that, even refusing to tell me the criteria that was used to determine how/why they came to that conclusion (even if it is an inaccurate one). They did say that a manager would be reaching out to me via email at their convenience but also asserted that they would be of no help to me. I have yet to hear anything, but apparently it wouldn't matter anyway. So, I'm wondering if there is any recourse for someone in my situation, or any sub-division or entity of ebay I should be trying to contact directly? Thank you in advance for your time and any suggestions that any of you may have.
title: Removal of Authentic Items

text:
Opinions are not Facts

Long story short, I listed 11 Golden State Warriors Kevin Durant Swingman Jerseys recently. How did I get these jerseys? My friend/co-worker used to be a Stadium Rep for Fanatics, he worked dozen homes games at the Oracle Arena. The Adidas Jerseys and apparel items are no longer contracted by NBA and it's Nike now. Also, Kevin Durant is no longer with the Warriors. I'm sure this is with all sports arenas across the world that you can't sell outdated jerseys so my friend took a box of genuine jerseys home and off of the store floor with the permission from Fanatics. I sold one to a customer but got a refund request because she thinks its a knock off. She said I scammed her because it was not an "Authentic" jersey. As some of you might know that all jersey manufacturers makes tier jerseys. Example, Authentic Game Jersey, mid tier jersey aka for Adidas is the Swingman and lastly, Replica. Obviously the Authentic game jerseys are made out quality materials that the players wear and sold at a premium price to the public. Mid Tiers are for the common people, cheaper materials used or different process to add logos but at a much affordable price; still nice jerseys. Replicas are the cheapest. Though Mid Tiers and Replicas are cheaper, it does not make them "Counterfeit". They are still sold in Stadiums and online. This customer keeps saying that I listed my item as "Authentic" and I scammed her because she got a lesser quality jersey and it is the worst counterfeited jersey she has ever seen. I messaged her numerous times that I did not use the word Authentic at all and always has been Genuine Swingman. Even sent her a link describing the differences between those different tiers. But she keeps insisting that it's a knockoff and wanting a refund. FIY, my listings have a no return policy. because I listed them at $29.99 with free s/h. That's a great deal even for an outdated jersey with a former player's name on it. Decided to sell it that cheap because they are obsolete in 2021. eBay, didn't even investigate, took my listing off, holding the funds and it looks like she gets to keep the jersey. So my gripe is why isn't eBay protecting sellers like me with 800+ Positive feedbacks at 100%? Why can't they investigate this and actually read the facts instead of listening to a customer's opinion? eBay has gone down hill the last few years and I'm getting sick of their automated system to handle cases like this. I can't even find the customer service phone to contact.
title: Opinions are not Facts

text:
Ebay Buyers Changing?

The Christmas season is wrapping up and I'm looking at my seasonal sales data. Now, I sell in a niche (hockey jerseys) but looking across platforms (Amazon, Fanatic, etc.) there are a few things that strike me. My pricepoint on most items is below what is the best price for the same item on Amazon and Fanatics, but my sell through doesn't reflect that. They sell multiples more of the same jersey at higher prices. This leads me to believe that shoppers have either lost confidence in Ebay for one reason or another, something about the Ebay platform is turning off casual shoppers, or something else is going on. I sell jerseys to newbies (less than 20 feedbacks) at low price points (less than $50 including shipping) and I get lowball offers from flipper wannabees. Items that have great value, but are priced in the $50-$150 range and retail in the $139-$300 are slow sellers. A decent jersey is going to last decades longer than a cheap replica, but they just don't generate volume like the cheapest jerseys do. Average transaction price sucks. Where did the shopper that understands quality go? What's going on?
title: Ebay Buyers Changing?

text:
Bidding process faulty.

I have experienced several instances where bidding appeared to continue for a few seconds beyond the -0- time left for bidding on an auction. I suspect that bidders are not placing their max bids until a few seconds before the zero time left ..to increase their chances to win. Their max bid doesn't register completely within the system for perhaps 5 seconds ..thus appearing that bidding is still happening past the 0 time left on the screen. At any rate I find this rediculous and discouraging for me to engage in more ebay auctions. A better..fairer system might be where bidders place their max bet out only once, initially, before a deadline, without being able to see other bids. The auction would end after the deadline for all bids.
title: Bidding process faulty.

By the time I quit (a power seller 15,000+ feedback) I had lost my major suppliers for drop shipping because they were requiring a brick and mortar store to stay as their customer. I deleted that account thinking I was never coming back. I didn't start out that way. I started by selling stuff in my house that I wanted to get rid of. I would buy at garage sales and resell here. That is my plan now. From what I have read this morning, I might be starting at the wrong time. LOL ... slow sales in March. Slow summer months, then school starts and people have less money to buy. So I am wondering, is the Christmas Season the only time people have good sales? I know it depends on what I sell. Once again I am clearing out my house of things I want to get rid of. A lot of them will be fixed price listings, a few 7-day auctions and I am still debating whether to get a store set up. It looks like the storefronts that use to be there are not and you really can't tell if I am in someone's store when I look at different items to buy. Sorry this is too long ... Just looking for any kind of feedback on what I have said here. I listed 7 items yesterday and plan on more this afternoon. I will be taking a lot of photos before that happens today. I just looked at a "preview" of this and it still shows my old profile pic and not the new one I added yesterday. But if I go to my dashboard the new profile pic shows up. I can handle criticism as well as suggestions. Thank you in advance for your comments and help.
title: I'm Back After Too Many Years

text:
Why is eBay overriding my Buyer Rule?

I had 3 auctions close pretty close together in the past hour. The first one was paid within 10 minutes or so, while the other 2 are not yet paid. But I see very different messages from eBay about their payment statuses. One is what I am used to and expect: Buyer has 4 days to pay. The other says it's an Auto-pay transaction, with the standard 1-hour latency for that. But I should not be having ANY such transactions, because I don't have it checkmarked in my Preferences. As much as I dislike slow payers and deadbeats, I DON'T use the autopay option with payment method required before bidding, because so many buyers hate it enough that they won't buy from sellers who require it, and it's even worse with auctions because there's no way they can do a snipe bid in time. When I run auctions, snipers can make a HUGE difference in the final winning bid amount, especially when there are multiple snipers. Case in point: the first auction. Its bid more than doubled from the $61 it had before the final few seconds. And that is how the fountain pen should have gone too. It had 917 views and 67 bleepin' watchers!!! But it had only one measley sniper try to win in the final seconds, leaving the zero feedback winner's proxy bid high enough to win. I have no idea how many of those other watchers might have TRIED to snipe, only to run into that *&^%#%@ payment method popup window! I DO NOT WANT THAT, so why the heck did eBay make it happen for this one auction??
title: Why is eBay overriding my Buyer Rule?

My recommendation is to never use credit cards to buy an ebay gift card. I cut and pasted the 3 ebay gift cards into an email to ebay. I do not think the employee read the email at all which said I used Capital One Shopping rewards to but them. Ebay could contact Capital One Shopping to verify I purchased ebay gift cards. Ebay did not do this because it believes providing lousy customer service will help it steal from customers. I am 56 years and not tech savvy. I do not know how to do what I have been asked. At that point I have to pay additional postage and occasionally use multiple envelopes or USPS ground. text:
What can I do - bad seller

I did a BIN purchase of a Funko pop, and in this collectable world, event stickers mean a lot. Seller advertised the item using an internet stock photo, not an actual photo of item they had, as they claimed they were setting up the auction for items pre-ordered. It was supposed to have the sticker from a specific specific retail event, but instead had the generic Funko Special Edition sticker. From Funko's website it even stated that retailer stock all had retailer stickers, and that on Funko's website they were exclusively selling the same thing and even noted that items from Funko store only would have some with Funko's sticker and some with other retailers event sticker and that there was no way to guarantee which they shipped. When my package arrived, it was stickered with Funko's sticker, not the retailer event as the stock photo showed and as the seller promised. I left a neutral rating explaining the difference and included an actual picture of what was in the box that they sent me as evidence. The seller has been continuously sending me messages that there is no way that happened as they only had the retailer even stickered items in their inventory and they hand checked every shipment going out, and then went so far to ask me if I had bought one from somewhere else. NO. That was the only time I ever bought that specific Funko character ever and it was from them, and at this point I am very insulted that they are insinuating that I am a liar and trying to swap things. After multiple message exchanges and more insinuating that I am a liar they finally said the only thing they can suggest is that I start a return. My questions are: 1) their listing said no returns, will it still let me do this? 2) what about shipping? I feel I should get all my money back from the entire transaction AND be compensated for any actual shipping costs to get it back to them since they are the ones that caused this. 3) any further ways to report this shady practice and get assistance with eBay, and if so, what is the process?
title: What can I do - bad seller

We sent messages back and forth where they kept badgering me to lower the shipping that I was trying to make money on shipping (i kept them all). 13 dollars? Anyway I canceled the order and reported them to Ebay as they were real rude. I'm not aware of Ebay doing anything. In fact the person sent 1 negative feedback and 3 neutral feedbacks saying I wouldn't wait for the payment due to them being in the hospital due to covid. text:
Seller commits fraud, EBay protects them and prevents negative reviews.

I've been on EBay since the late 90s as both a buyer and seller and I have never had an experience like the one that has unfolded over the last few weeks. Its been both a comfort and very disheartening to see others on these forums having very similar scams happen to them. I'm telling my story here to vent and help people and warn you off from one seller in particular: I won a Jumbo Funko Boba Fett on July 27th. My bid was actually for $31 with $40 shipping and $4 tax. I know for a fact that you could buy this item direct from Funko for $100 with free shipping. The seller felt a $70 loss was not going to work for them, so they decided to work the EBay system. They sent me a bogus envelope to a bogus address with a bogus name to my zip code in hopes that they could claim it was "lost in the mail", give me my money back and relist the item for a higher price. The first red flag was when they shipped the item USPS instead of FedEx like it said on their auction. This is taking advantage of a weak tracking system by the post office. Post office tracking only lists the zip code where the item is heading. It does not give you the specific address or how much the item weighs. (The post office actually has this information internally, but they will not give it to the consumer - more on that later). It took a week to get to my town (August 5th). I got the notification and it said the item was placed in the mailbox. (Which was strange because this item is 11-12 pounds and two feet tall packaged). There was nothing in the mailbox so I ran down to the post office with my tracking and I was told that the package was not addressed to me or sent to my address and it weighed about three ounces. At this point I began texting the seller in earnest, some of it not nice. They simply replied that they were sorry it had been lost in the mail and once they spoke with the post office they would refund my money. I told them they never mailed the item I won and mentioned the size of the package. They continued to say that it was a problem with the post office and they would refund my money. I said I wanted the item they didn't mail. They refused to acknowledge this was what had happened. I called EBay customer service (via the chat on the website). The CSR took my information and I made it very clear that I did not want my money back and I wanted the funko and I wanted the seller investigated. She said she was opening two files, but I would have to give the seller three days before EBay could get further involved. I knew this was a waste of three days, but I decided to play by the rules, though I did file a report on the postal inspector website. Two days later, the seller relisted my item with a Buy It Now price of $150 and shipping of $30. $180 versus my $75. I immediately contact EBay customer service again. The CSR took my info again. Acknowledged that it had been relisted and asked what resolutio...
title: Seller commits fraud, EBay protects them and prevents negative reviews.

text:
Do you expect this to last forever?

I try to neither be pessimistic or optimistic, but realistic. I source from a lot of places. Estate Sales, Auctions, Garage Sales, etc. I started doing this at the age of 14 under my parents account way back in 2007. I used to go to auctions and fill up a car for under $100. Farms started consolidating and the farm auctions dried up around 2009-2012. Gone were the days of the entire household's contents being thrown into boxes and totes on 3-6 hay racks. Did REALLY well on electronics since most in attendance were older folks. You'd also sometimes find a $100-$1000 item at the bottom of a tote. The auctioneers might of been lazy, but antiques wasn't their niche, it was land, tractors, implements, etc. Was also one of the first people with a smartphone to be able to lookup sold comps. Around 2014, as if someone flipped a massive switch, video games dried up. I went from finding as many as 20-30 classic systems (Nintendo, Super Nintendo, Sega Genesis, etc) a year to 1-2. In the same vain, personal computers dried up as well. Too many people didn't want their personal info somehow getting out there. Old school Apple computers are a rarity anymore as well. The biggest hit was Covid. Half the auction companies in a 200 mile radius retired, the other half went online. When they started taking credit cards and began shipping nationwide, prices skyrocketed. Most beforehand only took cash or check. When I went to court a month ago over a bad landlord, there were TWO DOZEN people ahead of me who were being sued by credit card companies. Young adults being stupid? NOPE, all were 55-75 years old. Hoarding and buying junk from TV shows like QVC and Jewelry TV from what I heard from some. So I can imagine others are just buying en masse from online auctions too. Oh...if you are on social security or disability, they can't garnish your wages. They know they are "untouchable" and that is why they do it. Thrift stores have became way more selective with what goes onto the floor and what goes online in the last 5 years. I can't blame resellers for this (some do, which is incorrect) as places like Goodwill have been selling on their own sites as well as eBay since 1999 or so. They have just become greedier doing this, as with pricing as well. I'm not saying it's not impossible anymore, it's just increasingly becoming harder. I used to go to one sale or two and have enough for a week's worth of listings. Now I have to hit up 3-4 thrift stores, a dozen or so yard sales, 1-2 estate sales and 1-2 auctions a week just to make the same amount of sales. Since pretty much everyone under the age of 50 is online, more and more gets looked up. More and more never makes it to various souring channels. You also have a bit of wave of younger flippers muddying the waters, who often crash and burn because they see people with YouTube channels claiming to make a million a month selling Funko Pops and Vinyl Records, and don't realize 99% of these...
title: Do you expect this to last forever?

text:
I got a counterfeit item

I purchased a Funko recently and while I was placing it next to other boxes I noticed some details were off. I searched for the indicators for fake funkos and this one has them all. It's the Chandler Bing Funko with the little chick in his hand. - The box doesn't have enough definition , so the colors are off. I searched for the REAL box on the internet and the frame of the box is supposed to have like a background in two tones of blue with drawings related to Friends. The one I received doesn't have that, except in zones where the drawings were too big and there's a glimpse of color, but you can't distinguish any shapes. - The cutout of the box with the drawing of Chandler at the front is too thick. I thought this was an exaggeration from the internet, but I've checked all the rest of my boxes and none of them has that thick cutout. - Under the box there's supposed to have a batch number. Mine doesn't have it. - The Funko's paint job is not exceptional either. At first I didn't think too much of it because I've seen bad painting jobs from Funko. But this one is very bad. I made a submission to eBay reporting that as counterfeit, but they didn't request for any pictures or proof, so I don't know if I successfully reported my purchase and I will be able to be refunded, or if I just reported the publication that, by the way, ended when I purchased it. Do I have to wait to be contacted by eBay for more info and proof with pictures? Or did I report the purchase wrong? Thanks, Paula
title: I got a counterfeit item

FRAUD ---   sales on eBay

Need some help! I am a ebay seller and I sell coins. Recently sold a coin for $895. The coin did not arive at its destination. When purchasing postage for shipping the system told me that i was oly insured for $100 and did I want to purchase additional insurance, It is a lot of money so I did. I also paid usps a additionaal fee to get signature on delivery. The insurer Interstate Fire and Casuality Company (insurer) a subsidiary of Allianze Global Risk US Insurance Company issued a policy for the purpose of "Parcel Insurance" (through Parcel Insurance Plan (PIP) their agency. After receiving my premium and keeping it the insurer denied my claim stating their policy does not cover coins. When they offered their policy they were aware I was shipping a coin and its value. I accepted the offer , paid the premium (based on its value (sales price) ) and sent my package to the buyer in good faith! Offering me coverage to insure the coin, taking the premium and then claiming they dont cover the item sounds like one thing : FRAUD. 1. Has anyone out there had any similar problems? 2. Has anyone received payment for lost coins? 3. anyone else paying for non existance services? I have sold hundreds of coins all stating they were insured and in many cases paid for coverage above the standard $100 included coverage Many many times. The coins were delivered and the "insurer" kept the premiums for something they say they do not cover and services never received. THEFT? There are hundreds of other sellers being ripped off out there and we need to get together to stop these companies from doing this with impunity. There are at least three parties involved in and profiting from this scheme. USPS, eBay and multible insurance comanies and agents. Please help me gather information to stop them from ripping us off. Class action suit possible?
title: FRAUD ---   sales on eBay

text:
Interesting request for a discount...

So there was a coin, a week ago, at regular price. Someone asked about a deal, so I said I can give 10% off in an offer. Days past, they didn't take me up on it, life goes on, then I put the coins on sale (not quite 10% off, but pretty close, I calculate there's a $1 difference between the sale price now and what the offer would have been, eBay won't let me send an offer for such a small discount). Well low and behold, buyer contacts me and asks for the 10% off (but the coin is already on sale). I pointed out the coin is on sale now, when it goes back to regular price, I'll send an offer for 10% off, otherwise just buy it while it's on sale. Interesting to see how this plays out... it's kind of like the guy who contacted me about a bunch of silver and I gave him melt prices. I wanted it for less than melt. Then melt goes up by 25% and he contacts me and decides he'll take the coin at the price I offered. (It was too late anyway becuase they were all melted by that time, but don't people get that items that change in price a lot are time sensitive with the offer? I'm not going to give the same price if you wait months to agree to my price). C.
title: Interesting request for a discount...

text:
A recent purchase of US Coins from a posting in California turned out to be more counterfeit US

The two coins were US silver dollars and listed from California and took over three weeks to get to me starting from Jamica NY to Chicago to Florida then Kentucky then Tennessee then NC the package was from China and the coins were illegal counterfeits. This has happened more than once and each time EBAY AI resolution turns on the buyer NO REFUND and takes the Seller position. Well for the record : Applicable Statute Under 18 U.S.C. § 472 , it is a federal offense to pass, utter, publish, sell, attempt to pass or sell, bring into the United States, possess, or conceal any falsely made, forged, counterfeited, or altered obligation or security of the United States with intent to defraud . [law.justia.com] Although § 472 does not explicitly use the term “mailing,” the act of sending counterfeit currency through the mail constitutes an attempt to pass , utter , or deliver such counterfeit items, and is therefore prohibited under the statute. Federal authorities treat the use of the mail as a method of facilitating the underlying offense. Penalties Under Federal Law Violation of 18 U.S.C. § 472 carries significant criminal penalties. The statutory maximum is: Up to 20 years of imprisonment , A fine , or Both imprisonment and a fine . [law.justia.com] Therefore according to legal council and the Banking system support selling or listing US currency on Ebay is a violation. Shipping this in US mail is illegal and those telling you to send it back to them in China for a refund is illegal up to 20 years in prison for acting on behalf of the seller. Ebay is not interested in the resolution and makes it difficult to resolve such a problem on their new AI system which is BAD and worthless. Furthermore, as a buyer using Ebay for over 25 years this site has become more like an auction for scam artist from China. The fact that Ebay allows counterfeit currency to be sold and takes the position they did not know and shuts down the site but does not protect the buyer as they claim is a lack of responsibility. I believe the next step is to make everyone aware that the site has more scammers than ligit sellers and such Federal violations need to be reported to the US Government.
title: A recent purchase of US Coins from a posting in California turned out to be more counterfeit US

text:
Scams on Ebay

I was scammed on Ebay and the really know what they are doing. Scammer list item, takes payment, sends some unrelated item, files for a return to "give" refund based on some made up reason - example "problem with buyers address" not true - scammer sent to wrong address down the street hoping I would not get the unrelated worthless item, not what I ordered. Then to eBay it looks like I received it, but I can't contact Ebay about specific seller and item because seller has already put in for a cancellation. So we then have to wait for the refund. The refund doesn't happen because the seller no longer exists. So then have to report that I did not receive item, but it looks like to UPS and Ebay that I did. Ebay says seller has been contacted and has request, mean while another week will go by and results will be no response from seller. Seller no longer exists but now is long gone with my money. They have the system figured out, they stay one step ahead and know how the bots answer and follow up. The bot process is slow, and even know you know the second you receive the unrelated item that it is a scam - in this case a paper face mask was shipped instead of a $2700.00 professional stage musical keyboard. They are gone before there is a way to contact Ebay and report it. Goes like this: List Item Sell Item Ship cheap completely unrelated item so there is a paper trail of something sent and received Issue a cancellation of the item to lock up the system for a few days so the receiver/buyer can not immediately report the scam Then leave eBay with the buyers money locking up the system even longer, while system is looking into the refund because seller cancelled - they leave and it gives them more days while the system is processing refund System then says there is a problem with the refund So Buyer now has to file "did not receive" or "not as described" neither of which are the right choices. They need a way to report scam/fraud right away. there needs to be a choice that will allow the buyer to report immediately so that they can not have the time to get away with the money. Frustrating, as we wait for this to continue to playout with long periods of times waiting on what we already know. "contacted seller but got no response" when we know the seller has been gone off Ebay immediately after the sale, fraudulent shipping, cancellation, and refund request all issued by seller to build in time.
title: Scams on Ebay

text:
CENSURA A MI PORTAL DE  BÚSQUEDA POR APELAR EL RESULTADO DE EBAY EN UN ARTÍCULO QE NO RECIBÍ

Saludos señores. Solicito la ayuda de la comunidad sobre una censura parcial a mi portal de búsqueda en Ebay impuesta a mi usuario por el mismo Ebay o algún administrador de usuarios que puede estár operando fuera de las políticas de Ebay. Pregunto a la comunidad: Es esto justo y legal?. Qué debo hacer al respecto?. Para justificar la Censura Ebay pueden alegar que violenté alguna de sus políticas?. Tengo muchos años comprando de manera segura en Ebay, en estos años he realizado algunas reclamaciones y en las que no he tenido tuve razón he aceptado mi responsabilidad, pero ahora ha ocurrido un caso de reclamación en el que he sido limitado en mi portal de búsqueda de artículos. Todo comenzó con un artículo de muy pocos dólares que compré, el proveedor creó el seguimiento con un transportista que nunca había visto en más de 15 años en Ebay, me pareció raro, pero al final lo que importa es la entrega del paque. El día de la entrega se marcó en mi portal de Ebay como paquete recibido, sin embargo, la dirección marcada en la entrega se refería a la cuidad completa y no a mi dirección especifica, si realmente yo hubiera recibido el paquete eso no tubiera importancia, pero nunca lo recibí y procedí a realizar una reclamación a Ebay, a la cual me respondieron que era improcedente porque el proveedor había presentado pruebas de la entrega. La prueba fue la misma que Ebay tiene en el portal de mi usuario y se puede observar a simple vista que donde realizaron la entrega no es mi dirección, que es el nombre de la ciudad sin especificar la dirección, ni mi código postal, ni mi número de buzón, no tenía nada de esto y, por sobre todo, no recibí el artículo. Teniendo Ebay esta información en su plataforma ni siquiera confirmó mi argumento y desestimó mi caso; posteriormente apelé la decisión y le adjunté fotos de las mismas informaciones que están en mi portal de usuario, entonces procedieron a devolverme el dinero, pero después de esto me han censurado parciamentel la búsqueda de artículos en mi usuario, ahora de todo lo que busco de manera específica no hay coincidencias, no puedo encontrar nada de lo que de rutina he buscado en todos estos años. Tuve la sostecha que se trata de un usuario fraudulento, que vende artículos a precios muy baratos para que los compradores no inviertan su tiempo en hacer reclamaciones, esto no es ni justo ni legal, por el contrario, es una oportunidad para que Ebay tome medidas con este tipo de vendedores; en este caso la medida fue en mi contra. Por qué Ebay desestimó mi primera reclamación sin haber investigado antes en el mismo portl?. Por qué estoy parciamente censurado y no puedo encontrar nada en el buscador?. Quería Ebay que me quede callado y no reclame el paquete que no recibí?. Es correcto el procedimiento de Ebay?. Qué hice mal en este caso que provocó una censura en mi buscador?. Cómo debo proceder ante esto?. Por favor disc...
title: CENSURA A MI PORTAL DE  BÚSQUEDA POR APELAR EL RESULTADO DE EBAY EN UN ARTÍCULO QE NO RECIBÍ

text:
My first time being scammed.

Hey y'all, recently I purchased some perfume from an eBay seller, they provided me with a fedex tracking number. Wonderful, except that whole "Delivery Exception" thing. Being the annoying, and pestering buyer that I usually am, contacted the seller asking what the deal with FedEx was, they told me they were working on it, twice, one week apart. Hmm, this response seem fishy to you, who has the time to get em-dashes anyways? Give it perhaps another two weeks, and finally I check in again, to see, wow it was delivered a couple days ago, but now its a USPS tracking number. The original listing was removed for violating eBay's policies, yeah very funny. Then, I take a harder look at this USPS tracking info, it's completely different, but the final address is correct. FedEx said it shipped from NORTH HILLS, CA. But this USPS Package shipped from Ohio, now maybe geography isn't your strong suite, but this doesn't make any sense. Okay, let's do some more recon. Out of a hunch, the original package shipped from North Hills, California. This seller seems to be a "real" business, so let's find that registration from California. [Image Removed by Moderator] Well, that's funny, the company is registered in North Hills CA, not Ohio, I'm on to you, Batman! With all this evidence in hand, including the buyers ChatGPT response they were looking into it, I schedule a call to talk to eBay's customer support, which seems to be an Indian call center, but that's okay. The person on the other end was very reassuring and very helpful. He told me I would have to wait 3 business days for the seller to respond, which is where I'm at now. Conveniently, they appear to be away on break till April, enjoy your break! Any-who, this was the first time I've ever been scammed, and wow was that elaborate! Unfortunately for this seller, I have too much time, and way too much vengeance. I hope this story gave you a good chuckle, and I hope to provide some positive updates whenever I get some. [Removed by Moderator]
title: My first time being scammed.

text:
Profit margins are shrinking

Does anyone else feel that calculating final value fees on the total transaction amount—including shipping and tax—is excessive? Because I have to pay fees on money that immediately goes toward postage or tax authorities, my profit margins are shrinking. I strongly feel that eBay should only take fees off the listed price, as that is the actual revenue earned. "I would like to request clarification on eBay’s final value fee structure. It is my understanding that fees are calculated based on the total amount paid by the buyer (including shipping and sales tax), rather than just the listing price of the item. As I do not retain the funds for shipping costs or taxes, I find this fee structure to be inefficient for sellers. I believe fees should only be applied to the listing price. Could you explain the rationale behind charging fees on non-revenue amounts?"
title: Profit margins are shrinking

text:
Ebay charged International Fees to me when I’ve excluded all international shipping

I successfully countered this fee and was refunded by Ebay yesterday, but I wanted to let other sellers know that this occurred and Ebay is doing it as a matter of policy unless we all object. I sold a watch to someone in England a couple of years ago and was appalled at the high transaction fees for an overseas sale. Ebay calls this the “International Fee”. I promptly excluded all sales out of the country to avoid these fees in the future. Last week, I sold a watch to someone in Portland OR. I’m not sure if they used an international shipper or if that is their current address. At any rate, eBay charged me the International Fee for an out of the country sale, saying that the buyer’s registered address was in Canada. Um, NO. That is for the buyer to be assessed or eBay to absorb, but if I’ve excluded international sales, I’m not paying that fee. It’s not transparent, and I’ve done everything on my end to avoid it. I hope others will speak up and add their voices to object to this non-transparent fee. I question the legality of eBay making sellers pay this fee (I’m sure it’s done to encourage international sales) without their express consent. Thanks and have a great 2026!
title: Ebay charged International Fees to me when I’ve excluded all international shipping

text:
Study of a Double Fraud: Item Substitution & Shipping Label Abuse

Hello everyone,I am sharing a recent experience to warn other sellers about a "Double Fraud" tactic I just encountered. I have already opened a case with eBay, but I want to document the sequence of events here for the community.High-Value Discontinued ItemI sold a Microsoft Natural Ergonomic Keyboard 4000, which was Brand New and Factory Sealed. This is a discontinued model (v4.0), and its value lies entirely in it being unopened. The buyer opened a return request claiming the item was "defective". During our communication, they admitted they were looking to replace their "old unit" of the same model. The buyer returned the item using a massive, oversized box ($21" \times 15" \times 10"$). This triggered a "Dimensional Weight" charge of 19 lbs, resulting in a staggering $62.95 shipping fee for a keyboard that actually weighs less than 7 lbs. This is a clear abuse of the return shipping process to inflate costs for the seller. Upon receiving the package, I performed a recorded unboxing and discovered a blatant "Switch Fraud":Serial Number Mismatch: I shipped .Version Downgrade: I shipped a modern v4.0 (Model 1048). I received a very old, heavily used v1.0 (Model KU-0462).Condition: The returned unit has visible signs of heavy wear, skin oils on the palm rest, and dust.Current Status:I have refused the refund and opened a case with eBay. I have uploaded photos of:The original S/N vs. the returned S/N.The oversized box and the $62.95 receipt.The heavy wear on the substituted unit.Lesson for Sellers:Always document your Serial Numbers before shipping high-value electronics. Without the photo of the original S/N sticker, it would be much harder to prove that the buyer sent back their own old junk. Has anyone else dealt with this specific 'double fraud' combo of item substitution and shipping label abuse, and what steps did you take to ensure eBay closed the case in your favor?
title: Study of a Double Fraud: Item Substitution & Shipping Label Abuse

text:
Temporarily pausing US international shipping services to the Middle East

Due to the current conflict in the Middle East, most major US shipping carriers are experiencing disruptions in service into the region; countries affected include: Israel, Bahrain, UAE, Iraq, Kuwait, Oman, Lebanon, and Jordan. eBay is temporarily pausing sales from the United States to buyers with shipping addresses in these countries as we cannot ensure that these packages will reach their final destination. We are closely monitoring the developments and will update you as soon as soon as we are able to resume operations in the Middle East. You may be concerned about shipping delays for items already shipped. If you recently shipped an item, eBay will automatically protect your seller performance, including: Your late shipment rate “Item not received” cases due to late delivery as long as you uploaded tracking before the case was opened, and have a physical scan from the carrier Service metrics for open item not received cases Negative and neutral feedback protections If you have recently shipped an item using eBay International Shipping , you are automatically protected once we receive your item at our domestic shipping hub. As the situation evolves, we will provide updates as needed. Thank you for selling on eBay.
title: Temporarily pausing US international shipping services to the Middle East

text:
USPS First Class Envelope Extremely High Loss Rate

Hello, I am writing this due to the very high loss rate on envelopes / Long periods of time it has been taking to deliver these items. I understand this has nothing to do with Ebay, But I feel more can be done to let buyers know about this shipping method. Ebay shows these items will be delivered within 2-7 days. While some do fall into this timeline, there has been a large number that often take towards a month, do not ever get scanned, or never get delivered at all. Orders also show they are delivered when they reach the final sorter leaving customers confused as to why it says delivered and why it is not in their mailbox, adding more item not received requests being opened and the item actually being delivered shortly after. I attempted to extend the shipping time through Ebay but the only resolution they gave me was to push the handling time back. This would remove me from being a top rated seller and still does not fix the unrealistic estimation given by USPS. It appears to come in waves where USPS does not process any of the envelopes I dropped off on random days then they get scanned a week later or not at all. Is there anything Ebay can do to teach buyers about this tracking to avoid questions I am constantly answering, claims being opened that should not be a claim, and hold USPS responsible for not processing these envelopes. (the items I am shipping are often stickers and iron on patches which are machinable so this should not be the issue.)
title: USPS First Class Envelope Extremely High Loss Rate

text:
Buying from low volume, low cost sellers a disservice to them?

The Community may respond to this by saying it is my choice to buy, or a seller's choice to become a seller, but that is simplistic. I had no idea how little - if any - profit is made by sellers. Is eBay banking on new or low-volume sellers not understanding the fee structure because concrete examples are not given ? I would rather know that a seller will realize at least a few dollars in profit, rather than receive the incredibly-helpful support and protections for Buyers. The answer may be that I ask any seller beforehand if they are know of the cost to them for selling me a $10 item, for example.
title: Buying from low volume, low cost sellers a disservice to them?

text:
Seller account dropped to Below Standard after 9 out-of-stock cancellations – what can I do?

Hello everyone, I’m looking for advice regarding my seller account. Since I started selling on eBay my account has always been clean and I never had any issues with buyers or defects. Recently I received 9 orders for the same item, but unfortunately the product became out of stock from my supplier and the supplier delayed restocking. Because of this I had no choice but to cancel the 9 orders. As a result I now have 9 defects on my account and my seller level dropped to Below Standard. Currently my account is restricted and I am unable to list any new items, which is making it very difficult for me to continue selling. This was honestly a lesson for me and it is the first time something like this has happened. I have already improved the way I manage my inventory to make sure this does not happen again. My questions are: Is there anything I can do to recover from this situation faster? How long does it usually take to move from Below Standard back to Above Standard? Is there any way to restore my ability to list new items sooner? I would really appreciate any advice from experienced sellers. Thank you.
title: Seller account dropped to Below Standard after 9 out-of-stock cancellations – what can I do?
